useCases.title: AI-Powered Sprint Planning
useCases.summary: Engineering manager uses AI to plan a 2-week sprint in under 30 minutes
useCases.description: Engineering manager uses AI to plan a 2-week sprint in under 30 minutes

scenario.context: Sprint planning meeting — team needs to commit to next sprint's work
scenario.before: EM manually reviews backlog, estimates capacity, assigns tasks — takes 3+ hours
scenario.after: AI suggests sprint backlog in 10 seconds, EM adjusts in 20 minutes
scenario.impact: Sprint planning time reduced by 80%, better capacity utilization

useCases.trigger: Engineering Manager opens the Sprint Planning view at the start of sprint planning meeting
preconditions: Team has completed at least 3 sprints with TaskFlow
AI scoring model is trained (50+ completed tasks)
postconditions: Sprint backlog is committed with assigned stories
Team members receive notifications of their sprint assignments

mainFlow.action: System shows team capacity (accounting for PTO, meetings)
AI proposes backlog with assignments, respecting dependencies and skills
EM reviews, adjusts 2-3 assignments, and commits sprint

alternativeFlows.name: Insufficient Data for AI
steps: System detects insufficient training data (< 50 tasks)
System falls back to rule-based suggestions sorted by deadline and manual priority
EM receives a note explaining the fallback and how to enable full AI

exceptionFlows.name: OpenAI API Unavailable
exceptionFlows.trigger: OpenAI API returns 503 or times out
exceptionFlows.handling: System uses cached scores if available (< 1 hour old), otherwise falls back to rule-based scoring. EM is notified with a banner.

businessRules: AI must respect team member capacity limits
No story can be assigned to a team member on PTO
useCases.notes: Key differentiator vs. Jira — this is what we demo to investors.
